commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a new RFC document proposing Gmail Pub/Sub pull delivery as OpenClaw's first-party Gmail notification path, including Gog/OpenClaw/downstream ownership, ack/retry behavior, credentials, setup, session policy, and validation expectations.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a design RFC for a new Gmail delivery mode, not a bug report with a current-main failure to reproduce.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainers need to review the RFC direction and compatibility contract; there is no narrow automated code repair to apply in this repository.

target.title: RFC 0009: Gmail Pub/Sub pull delivery
target.body:
Human written summary (clankers - dont touch this):
This RFC adds support for Gmail watch mode in `gog` and openclaw using a pull model. Currently gmail watch mode requires you to run a webhook URL for Google PubSub to push stuff to, which means your OpenClaw is exposed to the internet. A pull model eliminates that - you simply poll the PubSub queue and no longer need to listen on the internet, along with the obvious security improvements.

This will require changes in gog to add the pull model, OpenClaw to use this as default for gmail; docs for documenting it, and downstream consumers like nix-openclaw should also have this supported as first party config. 

AI slop below:

# Changes
- Add RFC 0009 proposing Gmail Pub/Sub pull delivery as OpenClaw's first-party production path.
- Define the intended repo boundary: `gog` owns Gmail/Pub/Sub runtime behavior, OpenClaw owns watcher supervision and hook dispatch, downstream packagers render coherent declarative config.
- Document ack/retry/lease behavior, setup behavior, credential handling, session policy, validation proof, and why push remains explicit compatibility rather than the default.

# Tests
- `git diff --check` - passed.

# Risks
- The RFC number is intentionally `0009` because open PRs currently reserve competing `0007` filenames; maintainers may prefer renumbering when the queue settles.
- This is design-only; implementation still needs `gog`, OpenClaw, and downstream packaging PRs.

# Follow-ups
- Resolve open questions in the RFC, especially hook-failure ack policy, Pub/Sub credential source, and setup ownership.
- If accepted, implement in order: `gog gmail watch pull`, OpenClaw delivery-mode config/watcher changes, gog package bump, then nix-openclaw high-level config.
target.author: joshp123
